message:
Harden MVCC EBR lifecycle, clippy-clean core/VDBE/pager/btree, and sweep harness quality gates

MVCC (fsqlite-mvcc):
- ebr: add nested_version_guards_pin_and_unpin_independently test proving
  inner/outer guard independence and correct metric accounting
- lifecycle: add test_version_guard_deferred_value_freed_after_abort verifying
  aborted txn retirements are reclaimed after unpin + epoch advance; add
  test_concurrent_same_page_writers_preserve_all_committed_versions proving
  8-thread same-page contention retains full version chain
- invariants: fix TxnManager::alloc_commit_seq to avoid holding lock across
  fetch_add; add explicit drop(active) in finish_commit_seq to release lock
  before stable_commit_seq update; enrich Debug impls with active_commits,
  stable_commit_seq, and guard_registry fields
- core_types: replace manual generation-mismatch panic with assert! macro;
  add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] on try_cleanup_orphaned_slot
- gc: relax EBR metric assertions from >= N+1 to > N for robustness

Core (fsqlite-core):
- connection: remove unnecessary .clone() on update_cols (TriggerEvent::Update);
  add #[allow(clippy::unused_self)] on map_insert_source_row_to_table_row
- por: wrap truncation cast in #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] block
- repair_engine: eliminate redundant data.clone() before corruption; use
  f64::from() instead of `as f64` casts for lossless numeric conversion

B-tree (fsqlite-btree):
- btree_invariant_tests: use HashSet::insert() return value instead of
  separate contains+insert; suppress unused Result from cache.insert() with
  let _ binding across 4 test sites

Pager (fsqlite-pager):
- arc_cache: enrich ArcCacheInner Debug impl with hits, misses, ghost_hits_b1,
  ghost_hits_b2, evictions_t1, evictions_t2, version_coalesce_count, admits
- pager: add explicit drop(inner) after lock guard usage in 3 test sites;
  add #[allow(clippy::similar_names, clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] on
  test_cache_eviction_under_pressure

VDBE (fsqlite-vdbe):
- vectorized_dispatch: replace &[tasks.clone()] with std::slice::from_ref;
  use map_or_else instead of map+unwrap_or_else for artifact path; add
  #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] on morsel_dispatch_e2e_replay_emits_artifact
- vectorized_ops: add #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] on 2 filter
  tests; replace "delta".as_bytes() with b"delta"; eliminate redundant .clone()
  on batch before apply_selection
- vectorized_prop_tests: eliminate 5 redundant .clone() calls on values/sorted
  vectors; replace nested match-continue with let-else patterns for Option
  destructuring; use iter().flatten() instead of filter_map for semi-join keys;
  replace match-Err-return with let-else for agg_result

Types (fsqlite-types):
- cx: add #[allow(clippy::redundant_clone)] on test_clone_propagates_trace_ids

Harness (fsqlite-harness, ~40 modules + 12 test files):
- adversarial_search: add cast_possible_truncation allow; simplify
  classify_flip_severity match arms; use mul_add for injection_rate
- backlog_quality_gate: major refactor of gate evaluation and formatting logic
- bin/: clippy fixes across durability_matrix_manifest, perf_baseline_pack_runner,
  scenario_coverage_drift_gate_runner, unit_coverage_drift_gate_runner,
  validation_manifest_runner
- ci_coverage_gate, ci_gate_matrix, confidence_gates: lint suppressions and
  minor refactors
- corpus_ingest, crash_recovery_parity, cross_process_crash_harness: allow
  attrs for truncation casts and similar names
- drift_monitor, e2e_log_schema, e2e_logging_init: clippy fixes for cast
  safety and redundant operations
- execution_waves, extension_parity_matrix, failure_bundle: lint allows
- fault_vfs: fix unused result
- forensics_navigator, fslab: simplify control flow, remove dead code
- impact_graph, isomorphism_proof, lane_selector: refactor match patterns
  and iterator chains
- lock_txn_parity, maintenance_parity: add lint allows for new clippy lints
- metamorphic: cast safety and truncation allows
- oracle: fix unused result binding
- performance_regression_detector, realdb_e2e_logging: minor lint fixes
- release_certificate, replay_harness, replay_triage: clippy compliance
- soak_executor, sql_pipeline_optimization, sql_pipeline_suites: refactors
- sql_semantic_differential, supervision: lint fixes
- t6sv2_checklist: significant simplification of checklist evaluation logic
- tcl_conformance, unit_fixtures, validation_manifest: clippy compliance
- wal_journal_parity: add lint allows
- 12 test files: misc lint allows and minor fixes

Beads:
- issues.jsonl: update bd-2y306.6 status to in_progress with progress notes
  documenting EBR test coverage additions and validation results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -694,7 +694,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2y306.3","title":"Tune epoch advance frequency for tail latency","description":"Tune the epoch advance frequency to balance GC throughput against tail latency. Epoch advance is a global synchronization point — advancing too frequently adds overhead, too infrequently wastes memory.

Tuning dimensions:
1. Epoch advance interval: time-based (default 100ms) or operation-count-based (default 1000 operations)
2. Collection batch size: how many retired versions to process per collection cycle
3. Background vs inline collection: collect in background thread vs inline during transaction operations

Measurements:
- GC pause duration at p50, p95, p99
- Memory reclamation latency (time from version retirement to actual free)
- Impact on transaction commit latency (should be < 1% at p50, < 5% at p99)
- Memory overhead vs advance frequency (plot the tradeoff curve)

Expected tradeoff: faster epoch advance -> lower memory usage, higher synchronization overhead. Find the Pareto-optimal point for typical workloads (OLTP: many small transactions; OLAP: few long transactions; mixed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configurable epoch advance interval (time and operation-count)
- [ ] Background collection thread option
- [ ] GC pause p99 < 1ms for typical workloads
- [ ] Memory reclamation latency documented
- [ ] Tradeoff curve documented for different advance frequencies
- [ ] Default settings work well for mixed OLTP/OLAP workloads","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:22.491568073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:17.170780990Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","mvcc","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2y306.4","title":"Benchmark: version chain memory under concurrent writers","description":"Benchmark version chain memory usage under concurrent writer workloads to validate EBR effectiveness. This proves that EBR actually bounds memory growth.

Workloads:
1. Single hot page: 100 concurrent writers updating the same page (worst case for chain growth)
2. Uniform random: 100 concurrent writers updating random pages from a 10000-page database
3. Zipfian skewed: 100 concurrent writers with Zipfian key distribution (realistic access pattern)
4. Long reader + writers: one long-running reader (holds epoch pin) while 100 writers update continuously

Metrics:
- Peak memory usage over time (should plateau, not grow linearly)
- Version chain length distribution (max, p50, p95, p99)
- GC reclamation rate (versions freed per second)
- GC-induced backpressure events (should be rare for uniform, common for single hot page)
- Memory overhead ratio (actual memory / minimum required memory)

Comparison: run same workloads WITHOUT EBR (version chains never freed) to show the memory explosion that EBR prevents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 workloads benchmarked
- [ ] Memory plateaus (not unbounded growth) for workloads 1-3
- [ ] Long reader impact documented (workload 4 shows bounded memory if reader completes)
- [ ] Comparison: EBR vs no-EBR memory growth chart
- [ ] GC metrics logged and analyzed
- [ ] CI integration: memory growth regression detection","acceptance_criteria":"1. Benchmark harness specifies workload, dataset shape, seed, and environment controls, and reports throughput plus p50/p95/p99 latency against a stable baseline. 2. Correctness guard tests (unit/property) run before performance assertions so regressions never trade correctness for speed. 3. An end-to-end smoke scenario validates the benchmarked path in integrated engine mode and verifies comparable outputs. 4. Benchmark logs include hardware/runtime config, query/workload IDs, sample distributions, and artifact paths for independent reproduction.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:33.989594419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:08:51.718076576Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","benchmark","ev-gated","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2y306.5","title":"Property tests: EBR version chain invariants under concurrent access","description":"Property-based tests for EBR version chain garbage collection correctness using proptest. EBR has critical safety invariants that must hold under all concurrent access patterns.

## Invariants to Test

1. No use-after-free: A version that is still observable by any active transaction must never be freed. Formally: if any Guard G pinned at epoch E_pin exists, and version V was created at epoch E_create where E_create >= E_pin, then V must not be freed.

2. Eventual reclamation: Once all transactions that could observe a version have completed, the version must eventually be freed (no memory leaks). Test by running write-heavy workload with short-lived transactions and verifying memory plateaus.

3. Monotonic epoch advance: The global epoch counter must be monotonically increasing. No epoch regression.

4. Chain-length bound: Version chain length must never exceed MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH + 1 (the +1 is for the in-flight write that triggers eager GC).

5. Thread-local retirement correctness: Versions retired on thread T retirement list are eventually freed, even if thread T terminates.

6. Stale reader detection: A reader that pins an epoch for longer than STALE_READER_THRESHOLD triggers a warning (not an error, just observability).

## Alien CS Reference
Fraser 2004 Practical Lock-Freedom: epoch-based reclamation formalized with safety proofs. Brown 2015 Reclaiming Memory for Lock-Free Data Structures: comparative analysis of EBR vs hazard pointers vs reference counting, showing EBR achieves lowest overhead for epoch-aligned workloads. crossbeam-epoch implements the three-epoch protocol from Keir Fraser thesis.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use proptest for random transaction schedule generation
- Use crossbeam::epoch::Guard lifetime tracking to verify no use-after-free
- Integrate Miri (#[cfg(miri)]) for focused hot-page scenarios (Miri is slow, limit scope)
- Use std::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize for allocation/deallocation counting to detect leaks
- For thread lifecycle tests: use std::thread::scope for structured concurrency (avoids detached threads)
- Store proptest regression seeds in proptest-regressions/ directory

## Logging Requirements
- Log epoch advances with old/new epoch values at tracing::debug level
- Log GC collection cycles: versions_freed, versions_pending at tracing::debug level
- Log stale reader warnings with reader_epoch, current_epoch, duration at tracing::warn level
- Log invariant violations (if detected in property tests) at tracing::error level with full context

## Test Generators
- Random transaction schedules: begin/read/write/commit/abort sequences
- Variable transaction lifetimes: short (1 op), medium (10 ops), long (1000 ops)
- Variable write rates: from 1 writer to 100 concurrent writers
- Hot-page scenarios: all writes targeting same page
- Thread lifecycle: spawn and terminate threads during test

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 invariants tested with proptest
- [ ] 10000+ random schedules tested per invariant
- [ ] Miri clean for hot-page scenario
- [ ] Regression seeds stored
- [ ] Integrated into CI test suite
- [ ] No memory leaks detected (track allocation/deallocation counts)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit/property test matrix covers core invariants, edge cases, regression seeds, and negative-path behavior with deterministic fixtures. 2. At least one integrated end-to-end scenario validates the same capability in real file-backed execution and checks externally observable behavior. 3. Tests assert required structured logging/spans/metrics fields and causal traceability so failures are diagnosable without guesswork. 4. CI enforces deterministic outcomes, captures rich artifacts, and blocks merge on flake policy violations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:20:57.279723734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:09.466820825Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2y306.6","title":"Unit tests: EBR VersionGuard lifecycle and eager GC trigger","description":"Focused unit tests for the EBR VersionGuard lifecycle, eager GC triggering, and chain-length enforcement. These complement the property tests (bd-2y306.5) with deterministic, targeted scenarios.

## Test Categories

1. VersionGuard lifecycle: Create a VersionGuard (pin epoch), perform reads and writes, drop VersionGuard (unpin). Verify: (a) epoch is pinned during guard lifetime, (b) epoch is unpinned on drop, (c) nested guards work correctly.

2. Version retirement: Create a version, supersede it with a new version, verify the old version is placed in the retirement list. Then advance epoch and verify the old version is freed.

3. Eager GC trigger: Create MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH versions on a single page, verify that eager GC is triggered on the next write. Verify chain length is reduced after GC.

4. Backpressure: Create MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH versions with active readers holding all versions, attempt another write. Verify the writer is blocked (backpressure) until a reader drops its guard.

5. Stale reader detection: Create a VersionGuard, hold it for longer than STALE_READER_THRESHOLD, verify a warning is emitted. Verify the stale reader does not prevent GC of versions created after it.

6. Transaction abort cleanup: Begin a transaction, create versions, abort. Verify all versions created by the aborted transaction are retired immediately.

7. Concurrent version creation: Multiple threads create versions on the same page simultaneously. Verify all versions are correctly linked in the chain and no version is lost.

## Alien CS Reference
crossbeam-epoch documentation and implementation: three-epoch protocol ensures no use-after-free with deferred destruction. The eager GC trigger and chain-length bound are FrankenSQLite extensions beyond standard EBR.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use #[test] with direct crossbeam::epoch::pin() calls for lifecycle tests
- For backpressure test: use std::thread::spawn with barrier for synchronization, then verify writer blocks
- For stale reader: use std::thread::sleep(STALE_READER_THRESHOLD + margin) and capture tracing output
- Use tracing-test crate to assert on emitted log events (e.g., verify stale reader warning)
- Each test should be deterministic and complete in < 1 second

## Logging Requirements
- Log epoch pin/unpin at tracing::trace level for lifecycle verification
- Log GC trigger at tracing::debug level: chain_length, max_chain_length, versions_freed
- Log backpressure events at tracing::warn level: blocked_writer_thread, blocking_reader_epoch
- Log stale reader warnings at tracing::warn level: reader_epoch, current_epoch, hold_duration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 test categories implemented and passing
- [ ] Deterministic tests (no timing-dependent flakiness)
- [ ] Miri clean for lifecycle and retirement tests
- [ ] Backpressure correctly blocks writer and unblocks on reader drop
- [ ] Stale reader warning verified via tracing-test
- [ ] Concurrent version creation verified with 8+ threads
- [ ] All tests complete in < 5 seconds total","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit/property test matrix covers core invariants, edge cases, regression seeds, and negative-path behavior with deterministic fixtures. 2. At least one integrated end-to-end scenario validates the same capability in real file-backed execution and checks externally observable behavior. 3. Tests assert required structured logging/spans/metrics fields and causal traceability so failures are diagnosable without guesswork. 4. CI enforces deterministic outcomes, captures rich artifacts, and blocks merge on flake policy violations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:55.539570380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:07.051383448Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","memory-management","mvcc","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2y306.6","title":"Unit tests: EBR VersionGuard lifecycle and eager GC trigger","description":"Focused unit tests for the EBR VersionGuard lifecycle, eager GC triggering, and chain-length enforcement. These complement the property tests (bd-2y306.5) with deterministic, targeted scenarios.

## Test Categories

1. VersionGuard lifecycle: Create a VersionGuard (pin epoch), perform reads and writes, drop VersionGuard (unpin). Verify: (a) epoch is pinned during guard lifetime, (b) epoch is unpinned on drop, (c) nested guards work correctly.

2. Version retirement: Create a version, supersede it with a new version, verify the old version is placed in the retirement list. Then advance epoch and verify the old version is freed.

3. Eager GC trigger: Create MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH versions on a single page, verify that eager GC is triggered on the next write. Verify chain length is reduced after GC.

4. Backpressure: Create MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH versions with active readers holding all versions, attempt another write. Verify the writer is blocked (backpressure) until a reader drops its guard.

5. Stale reader detection: Create a VersionGuard, hold it for longer than STALE_READER_THRESHOLD, verify a warning is emitted. Verify the stale reader does not prevent GC of versions created after it.

6. Transaction abort cleanup: Begin a transaction, create versions, abort. Verify all versions created by the aborted transaction are retired immediately.

7. Concurrent version creation: Multiple threads create versions on the same page simultaneously. Verify all versions are correctly linked in the chain and no version is lost.

## Alien CS Reference

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use #[test] with direct crossbeam::epoch::pin() calls for lifecycle tests
- For backpressure test: use std::thread::spawn with barrier for synchronization, then verify writer blocks
- For stale reader: use std::thread::sleep(STALE_READER_THRESHOLD + margin) and capture tracing output
- Use tracing-test crate to assert on emitted log events (e.g., verify stale reader warning)
- Each test should be deterministic and complete in < 1 second

## Logging Requirements
- Log epoch pin/unpin at tracing::trace level for lifecycle verification
- Log GC trigger at tracing::debug level: chain_length, max_chain_length, versions_freed
- Log backpressure events at tracing::warn level: blocked_writer_thread, blocking_reader_epoch
- Log stale reader warnings at tracing::warn level: reader_epoch, current_epoch, hold_duration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 test categories implemented and passing
- [ ] Deterministic tests (no timing-dependent flakiness)
- [ ] Miri clean for lifecycle and retirement tests
- [ ] Backpressure correctly blocks writer and unblocks on reader drop

CI enforces deterministic outcomes, captures rich artifacts, and blocks merge on flake policy violations.","notes":"Progress (WindyThrush, 2026-02-18): added deterministic EBR coverage for nested guard pin/unpin lifecycle, abort-path deferred retirement reclamation, and concurrent same-page writer version-chain retention. Workspace-wide cargo fmt --check remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated diff in crates/fsqlite-harness/src/bin/unit_coverage_drift_gate_runner.rs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:55.539570380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:00:14.966586439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","memory-management","mvcc","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2y306.7","title":"Alien Contract: EBR Version-Chain GC rollout (budgeted + stale-reader safe-mode)","description":"Change: Finish and harden epoch-based reclamation rollout for MVCC version chains using VersionGuardTicket lifecycle, deferred retire semantics, and stale-reader detection budgets.\n\nHotspot evidence: open blockers bd-2y306.1/.2/.3/.4/.5/.6 plus triage unblocks indicate GC path is a system bottleneck and dependency root.\n\nMapped graveyard sections: §14.10 EBR, §0.15 tail decomposition, §0.19 evidence ledger, §0.20 CI gate blueprint.\n\nEV score: (5*4*5)/(3*2)=16.67\nPriority tier: S\nAdoption wedge: keep current GC semantics as conservative mode; EBR controls enabled behind PRAGMA/runtime flag during canary.\nBudgeted mode: max deferred retire queue per txn + stale pin warning budget + forced eager reclaim fallback on threshold breach.\nExpected-loss model: minimize data-race/UAF risk vs throughput loss; false-positive stale warnings tolerated to avoid unsafe reclamation.\nCalibration + fallback trigger: if stale-reader rate or memory watermark exceeds envelope for 3 windows, switch to eager sync reclaim mode and emit evidence event.\nIsomorphism proof plan: visibility + commit ordering unchanged; only reclamation timing changes post-visibility horizon.\np50/p95/p99 target: cut GC pause tails by 20/30/40% with zero invariant regressions.\nPrimary risk + countermeasure: latent lifetime bug -> miri + loom-style stress + property tests + deterministic replay harness.\nRepro artifact pack: benchmark manifests, mem profiles, invariant logs, replay seeds, .beads linkage.\nPrimary paper status: EBR primary paper checklist required before close.\nInterference test: required with concurrent lock-table/SSI tuning changes.\nDemo linkage: MVCC observability dashboard and GC chain-length tracker.\nRollback: runtime flag to disable EBR retire path and force conservative reclaim.\nBaseline comparator: current non-EBR or partially integrated reclaim path.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. EV/risk contract explicitly defines expected gain, acceptable risk envelope, and deterministic fallback triggers with non-negotiable safety bounds. 2. Unit/property tests validate trigger logic, guardrail calculations, and fallback-state invariants under boundary conditions. 3. End-to-end scripts validate both primary and fallback paths under load, crash, and recovery scenarios with deterministic seeds and replay instructions. 4. Detailed structured logging records decision variables, trigger reasons, fallback activation timeline, and post-fallback outcomes for full auditability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:29:39.760245909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:05.106810197Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ebr","ev-gated","mvcc","optimization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:29:39.760245909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2y306.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:30:10.376387998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yfi","title":"Implement DISTINCT for table-backed SELECT queries","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:47:46.359047875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:55:32.561065731Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:55:32.561046656Z","close_reason":"DISTINCT implemented via post-execution dedup in connection layer. Both table-backed and expression-only paths supported. 101 fsqlite + 626 core tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2257,"issue_id":"bd-2yfi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## DISTINCT support for table-backed SELECT

**Repro (current behavior):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER, flag INTEGER);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 1);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (2, 0);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (3, 1);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (4, 0);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (5, 1);
SELECT DISTINCT flag FROM t;
```
=> Returns 5 rows (all rows) instead of 2 (distinct values 0 and 1).

**Root cause:** Table-backed SELECT codegen (`codegen_select_full_scan`) does not emit
deduplication logic when `Distinctness::Distinct` is set. The AST `SelectCore::Select`
carries a `distinct: Distinctness` field that is currently ignored in the scan codegen.

**Possible implementations:**
1. Post-execution dedup in Connection layer (simple but O(n) memory)
2. Emit a sorter-based dedup in VDBE codegen (more correct, matches C SQLite approach)
3. Use a hash set in the engine for seen result rows

**Touchpoints:** `codegen_select_full_scan` in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs`,
or `execute_table_program` postprocessing in `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:48:00Z"},{"id":2258,"issue_id":"bd-2yfi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Resolution: bd-2yfi — DISTINCT implemented via post-execution dedup (IvoryWaterfall)

Implemented DISTINCT for both table-backed and expression-only SELECT queries via
post-execution row deduplication in the connection layer (`connection.rs`).

### Changes:
1. Added `is_distinct_select()` helper — checks `Distinctness` on the AST
2. Added `dedup_rows()` helper — removes duplicate `Row`s using `PartialEq`
3. Added `distinct: bool` field to `PreparedStatement` so prepared DISTINCT queries also work
4. Modified `execute_statement()` — applies dedup after both table-backed and expression-only execution
5. Modified `compile_and_wrap()` — sets the `distinct` flag for prepared statements
6. Removed DISTINCT rejection in `compile_expression_select()` — was returning NotImplemented error
7. Removed `#[ignore]` from `distinct_table_backed_select` test — now passes

### Approach rationale:
Used connection-layer post-execution dedup (approach 1 from the bead) because:
- `codegen.rs` was reserved by another agent (SageHawk)
- O(n) memory is acceptable for current development stage
- No new VDBE opcodes required
- Works for both table-backed and expression-only paths

### Verification:
- 101 fsqlite tests pass (including the previously-ignored DISTINCT test)
- 626 fsqlite-core tests pass
- Clippy clean (pedantic + nursery)
- cargo fmt clean
